subject: english
learning_objectives: Define tense as an aspect of the verb
Use each present tense correctly in sentences

sections.heading: Definition and Types
sections.content: Tense is an aspect of the verb that expresses the time of an action. The note treats four present-tense forms: simple present, present continuous, present perfect, and present perfect continuous.

sections.heading: Simple and Continuous Forms
sections.content:
Simple present expresses habitual action and universal truth: I eat rice. The earth is spherical.
Present continuous shows an action in progress and is formed with is/are plus the -ing form: She is cooking. We are playing football.

sections.heading: Perfect Forms
sections.content:
Present perfect is formed with has/have plus the participle: They have gone out. He has spoken to her.
Present perfect continuous uses has/have been plus the -ing form: I have been working. She has been singing.
